meta.note: Costs are approximate mid-2026 estimates in USD (accom per night; dailyLiving = food+local transport+activities for 2, excl. accommodation; rentMonthly = furnished 1-BR). Weather = climate normals: avg daily high/low C and monthly rainfall mm. Verify visas and live advisories before booking.

Thailand.visa: Visa-free 30 days (cut from 60 in 2026). Free TDAC arrival card <=72h at tdac.immigration.go.th. Extend +30d (~$55). Longer: DTV visa.
Thailand.bestTime: Nov-Feb (cool, dry). Avoid Mar-Apr heat; Chiang Mai haze Feb-Apr; monsoon Jun-Oct.
Thailand.safety: Very safe (L1). Tuk-tuk gem/tailor scams, fake closed-attraction touts. Avoid deep south + Cambodia border. Tourist Police 1155.

Vietnam.visa: Visa-free 45 days (Hungary, to Aug 2028). Or e-visa 90d multi ~$25-50 at evisa.gov.vn.
Vietnam.bestTime: Oct-Apr overall. Central (Da Nang/Hoi An) avoid Sep-Nov (typhoon/flood). North best Mar-Apr & Oct-Nov.
Vietnam.safety: Very safe (L1). Use Grab app; watch taxi-meter & Ha Long cruise scams.

Indonesia.visa: VOA/e-VOA ~$35, 30d, extend +30. Long-term: E33G remote-worker visa (1yr). Pay Bali tourist levy ~$10pp at lovebali.baliprov.go.id.
Indonesia.bestTime: Dry Apr-Oct (best May/Jun/Sep/Oct). Wet Nov-Mar (cheaper, greener).
Indonesia.safety: Stable. #1 risk = scooter accidents - ride only with licence + IDP. Scooter-damage & money-changer scams.

Malaysia.visa: Visa-free 90 days. Free MDAC >=3 days before arrival at imigresen-online.imi.gov.my. Long-term: DE Rantau nomad pass.
Malaysia.bestTime: KL/Penang driest Dec-Apr. Haze season Aug-Oct (Sumatra fires).
Malaysia.safety: Among SE Asia's safest. Bag-snatch/phone theft; use Grab.

Cambodia.visa: e-Visa ~$36 (evisa.gov.kh) or VOA ~$30, 30d, extend +30. USD used everywhere.
Cambodia.bestTime: Nov-Feb best (Angkor sunrise, peak crowds). Mar-Apr very hot. Flood risk Sep-Oct.
Cambodia.safety: Generally safe. Buy Angkor pass ONLY at angkorenterprise.gov.kh. Pub St drink-spiking; bag-snatch.

Laos.visa: 30-day visa exemption currently in effect for Hungary/EU (temporary, ~to 2028 - VERIFY live). Else e-visa ~$50 or VOA ~$30-40, 30d. Exemption non-extendable (border hop).
Laos.bestTime: Nov-Feb (cool, dry). AVOID Mar-Apr burning/haze season (esp. Luang Prabang). May-Oct monsoon.
Laos.safety: Generally safe, low violent crime. Petty theft, road accidents; tuk-tuk overcharging (no ride-hail, negotiate).

Singapore.visa: Visa-free 90 days. Free SG Arrival Card <=72h at ica.gov.sg.
Singapore.bestTime: Feb-Apr driest. Hot/humid year-round. Haze risk Jun-Oct.
Singapore.safety: Among world's safest BUT strict: drugs=death penalty, vapes banned (even transit), fines for gum/jaywalk/litter.

Japan.visa: Visa-free 90 days. Pre-register Visit Japan Web.
Japan.bestTime: Apr-May & Oct-Nov best. Cherry blossom late Mar-early Apr; avoid Golden Week; rainy Jun-mid-Jul; typhoons Aug-Sep.
Japan.safety: Very safe. Rare nightlife bar scams in Kabukicho/Roppongi - never follow touts.

South Korea.visa: Visa-free 90 days. No K-ETA for Hungary (to end-2026). Free e-Arrival Card.
South Korea.bestTime: Apr-May & mid-Oct-early Nov. Monsoon mid-Jun-Jul; typhoons Aug-Sep; spring yellow dust.
South Korea.safety: Very safe. Use Kakao T for taxis; beware K-pop ticket fraud.

Taiwan.bestTime: Oct-Nov & April. Plum rain May-Jun; typhoons Jul-Sep; hot humid summers.
Taiwan.safety: Very safe. No meat imports (ASF fines). Traffic + quakes/typhoons are the main risks.

Hong Kong.bestTime: Oct-early Dec (cool, dry). Typhoons May-Nov (T8 shuts the city); humid summers.
Hong Kong.safety: Among safest cities. Kowloon electronics/jewelry bait-and-switch; taxi steering.

China.visa: CHANGES OFTEN - verify with Embassy. Unilateral visa-free up to 30 days for Hungary (to Dec 31 2026), free. Else 240h transit. Excludes Tibet/Xinjiang.
China.bestTime: Apr-May & Sep-Oct (avoid Oct 1-7). Shanghai plum rains mid-Jun-Jul; Chengdu often grey.
China.safety: Safe for crime; risk is scams (infamous tea-house scam - never follow strangers). Google/WhatsApp/Booking blocked - use foreign eSIM + Alipay/WeChat/Trip.com/Didi.

India.visa: e-Tourist Visa online only (no VOA). 30d $10-25 / 1yr multi $40 (+3% fee), max 180d/yr. indianvisaonline.gov.in.
India.bestTime: Oct-Mar. Avoid Apr-Jun heat & Jul-Sep monsoon. Delhi smog late Oct-Jan (bring N95).
India.safety: Moderate. Meter/closed-hotel/gem scams. Women: dress modestly, Uber/Ola at night.

Nepal.visa: Visa on arrival: 15d $30 / 30d $50 / 90d $125 (cash). Max 150d/yr. Solo trekking banned - guide + TIMS required.
Nepal.safety: Very safe. Trek-agency & taxi overcharging - agree fares up front.

Sri Lanka.visa: ETA required online ~$50, 30d double-entry (verify free-ETA list at eta.gov.lk - Hungary not confirmed). Extend to 90-180d.
Sri Lanka.bestTime: Dec-Mar (south/west + hills). Two monsoons - opposite coast is always dry.
Sri Lanka.safety: Safe, friendly. Tuk-tuk & gem scams; drink-spiking reported. Use PickMe app.

Bangladesh.visa: Visa required. VOA ~$51pp cash (USD/EUR), 30d, extend +30, at officer discretion - safer to pre-arrange. e-visa announced but unreliable (verify visa.gov.bd).
Bangladesh.bestTime: Nov-Feb (cool, dry). Apr-May very hot; Jun-Oct monsoon/flooding. Cox's Bazar beach Nov-early Mar.
Bangladesh.safety: US advisory Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) - crime, unrest; avoid demos. DO NOT travel Chittagong Hill Tracts. Dress conservatively; use Pathao/Uber. Check travel.state.gov.

Austria.visa: EU freedom of movement: Hungarian/EU citizens need no visa and no ETIAS for any length of stay. Enter and reside freely with a valid passport or national ID card.
Austria.bestTime: May-September for warm weather and Alpine hiking; December-March for ski season. Spring and early autumn offer mild weather and fewer crowds.
Austria.safety: Very safe. Low violent crime; main risk is petty theft/pickpocketing in tourist areas of Vienna and Salzburg. Excellent healthcare and infrastructure.

Philippines.visa: Visa-free for up to 30 days for Hungarian/EU passport holders. Passport must be valid at least 6 months beyond stay; onward/return ticket may be requested. Extensions available locally via the Bureau of Immigration.
Philippines.bestTime: December-April (dry season) is ideal, especially January-March. Avoid June-October typhoon and rainy season; February-April best for beaches and diving.
Philippines.safety: Generally safe in tourist areas (Cebu, Palawan, Boracay, Manila tourist zones). Petty theft and scams occur in cities. Avoid the Sulu Archipelago and parts of western/central Mindanao due to insurgency and kidnapping risk. Watch sea conditions during typhoon season.

weather.hazard: Hot humid year-round; SW monsoon May-Sep, second peak Oct-Nov; best months Dec-Mar (west/south coast dry).

cities.food: Rice & curry $2-4 / Casual for 2 $10-18 / Mid dinner $22-40 / Beer $2.5-4.5 / Coffee $2-3
cities.transport: Scenic train Kandy-Ella $0.5-23 by class / Short hops $1-3 / Tuk-tuk $1-3
cities.internet: Dialog/Mobitel SIM $4-7 / Coworking sparse (guesthouse Wi-Fi + cafes)

weather.hazard: Cool hill country (cooler nights at altitude); wettest Oct-Nov (NE monsoon) and Apr; best months Jan-Mar (driest, clear hill views).

cities.food: Local $1.5-3 / Casual for 2 $6-10 / Mid dinner $20-35 / Tea $0.2-0.5 / Coffee $2.5-4
cities.transport: CNG/rickshaw $0.30-3 (negotiate) / Pathao+Uber ~$3-4 / Brutal traffic - allow extra time
cities.internet: eSIM $15-28/10-20GB / Coworking sparse (Moar). Power cuts - bring backup. Light/async work only

landmarks.name: Sadarghat River Port
landmarks.why: Asia's busiest river port, raw Dhaka

weather.hazard: Monsoon Jun-Sep (flooding common); pre-monsoon storms Apr-May; hot humid summer; best months Nov-Feb (cool, dry).

cities.food: Local $1.6-3.2 / Casual for 2 $6-10 / Mid seafood dinner $15-30 / Tea $0.2-0.5 / Coffee $2.5-4
cities.transport: CNG/tomtom $0.50-2 / Rickshaw $0.50-1.50 / Weak ride-hail - use local autos
cities.internet: Good 4G/eSIM / Coworking: none (work from hotel). Workation for light work only

landmarks.name: Inani Beach
landmarks.why: Golden sand, coral rocks, calm water

weather.hazard: Very heavy coastal monsoon Jun-Sep; cyclone risk Apr-May and Oct-Nov (Bay of Bengal); best months Nov-Mar (dry beach season).

cities.food: Sausage at a Würstelstand ~€4-5; daily lunch menu (Mittagsmenü) ~€12-15/person; casual dinner for 2 with drinks ~€55-70; mid-range Wiener Schnitzel dinner for 2 ~€90-110; local beer (0.5L) ~€4.50; melange coffee in a classic café ~€4.50.
cities.transport: Excellent Wiener Linien metro/tram/bus network: single ride €2.60, 24h pass €8, 72h pass €17.10; the 365-day annual pass is famously €365. Bolt/Uber widely available, short city ride ~€9-14. Mostly walkable and very bike-friendly.
cities.internet: Austria/EU eSIM cheap: ~$4-10 for 5-10GB (Airalo/Saily); local SACK/Hot Tariff SIMs offer big data bundles. Fast, reliable fiber everywhere. Coworking: Impact Hub day pass ~€20, monthly from ~€75; Zoku day pass ~€38; premium hot-desk memberships ~€125-240/month.

landmarks.name: Schönbrunn Palace & Gardens
landmarks.why: Former Habsburg summer residence with vast baroque gardens, Gloriette and the world's oldest zoo
landmarks.when: Early morning to beat crowds; gardens free to roam
landmarks.how: U4 metro to Schönbrunn, ~15 min from centre

landmarks.name: St. Stephen's Cathedral (Stephansdom)
landmarks.why: Gothic landmark in the heart of the old town with a tiled roof and tower climb over the city
landmarks.when: Anytime; climb the south tower for views

landmarks.name: Belvedere Palace (Klimt's The Kiss)
landmarks.why: Baroque palace housing Gustav Klimt's The Kiss and Austrian art, with terraced gardens
landmarks.how: Tram D or short walk from centre

landmarks.name: Naschmarkt & MuseumsQuartier
landmarks.why: Lively food market for cheap eats, plus a cultural courtyard of museums and cafés to work/relax
landmarks.when: Naschmarkt mornings (closed Sundays); MQ courtyard evenings

weather.hazard: Best months May-Jun and Sep-Oct (mild, sunny, fewer crowds). Jul-Aug warm and the wettest (thunderstorms); winters cold, grey and damp with occasional snow Dec-Feb. No monsoon/typhoon risk.

cities.food: Street/market Thai meal $2-4 (60-120 THB); casual sit-down dinner for 2 ~$16-22; mid-range Western/seafood dinner for 2 ~$45-60; local Chang/Leo beer $2-4 at a shop, $3-5 in a bar; Western cafe coffee $2.50-4.50 (90-160 THB).
cities.transport: No real public transit beyond slow songthaews (~$1/30 THB). Grab/Bolt is the nomad default: short hop $3-5, airport to Patong ~$20-25. Tuk-tuks are tourist-priced ($10-18 even short) - avoid or haggle hard. Most renters get a scooter: $90-150/month plus ~$5 to fill the tank.
cities.internet: Fibre in condos is fast and cheap (often bundled in rent, otherwise ~$15-20/mo). Tourist eSIM/SIM: Airalo 10GB ~$11/30 days, or local AIS/True tourist SIM 299-599 THB ($9-18) with generous data. Coworking is plentiful (Bangtao, Rawai, Chalong): day pass 150-400 THB ($4-11), monthly hot desk ~$80-150.

landmarks.name: Big Buddha (Phra Phutthamingmongkhon)
landmarks.why: 45m marble-clad hilltop Buddha with 360-degree island and coastline views; one of Phuket's signature sights.
landmarks.when: Early morning to beat heat and crowds; clear dry-season days for views.
landmarks.how: 20-30 min by Grab/scooter from Phuket Town or Chalong up a winding hill road.

landmarks.name: Phuket Old Town (Thalang Road)
landmarks.why: Sino-Portuguese shophouse streets with cafes, street art, galleries and a lively Sunday Walking Street night market - the most workable, atmospheric base for nomads.
landmarks.when: Late afternoon into evening; Sunday for the walking-street market.
landmarks.how: Central; walkable, or short Grab ride from anywhere in the city.

landmarks.name: Phi Phi Islands day trip
landmarks.why: Iconic limestone cliffs, Maya Bay and snorkelling lagoons - the classic Andaman boat excursion.
landmarks.when: Dry season Nov-Apr for calm seas; book early-departure speedboat tours.
landmarks.how: Speedboat/ferry from Rassada Pier; most book a guided day tour with hotel pickup.
landmarks.cost: $35-55 pp tour incl. lunch + national park fee

landmarks.name: Kata & Karon Beaches
landmarks.why: Long, swimmable west-coast sand beaches with sunset bars and surf - the relaxed counterpoint to busy Patong.
landmarks.when: Dry season for swimming; rainy-season swells suit surfers.
landmarks.how: 15-25 min by Grab or scooter from Phuket Town.

weather.hazard: Best months Dec-Mar (dry, calm seas, lower humidity). Southwest monsoon May-Oct brings heavy rain, big swells and frequent red-flag/rip-current beach closures - Sep-Oct wettest. Hot and humid year-round (highs 31-34C).

cities.food: Street pad thai or khao gaeng 40-70 THB ($1.20-2); casual Thai dinner for two 250-400 THB ($7-12); mid-range Western/seafood dinner for two with drinks 700-1,200 THB ($20-35); local beer 70-90 THB ($2-2.60); coffee 60-80 THB ($1.80-2.40).
cities.transport: No mass transit; songthaew shared trucks 30-60 THB per ride. Scooter rental 200-300 THB/day ($6-9) is the nomad norm. Grab works in Krabi Town/Ao Nang: short hop 80-150 THB, airport to Ao Nang ~500-600 THB ($15-18). Longtail boat to Railay ~150-200 THB pp.
cities.internet: Tourist eSIM (AIS/TrueMove) ~$8-15 for 15-30GB. Fiber in condos is reliable (50-200 Mbps). Few dedicated coworking spaces; cafes in Ao Nang and Krabi Town serve as workspots, plus a handful of small coworking/coliving options around 150-250 THB/day.

landmarks.name: Railay Beach
landmarks.why: Iconic limestone-cliff peninsula reachable only by boat, with world-class rock climbing and turquoise swimming bays.
landmarks.when: Dry season Dec-Mar, mornings before crowds
landmarks.how: Longtail boat from Ao Nang (~150-200 THB pp, 15 min)
landmarks.cost: Boat ~$5 pp round trip; beach free

landmarks.name: Four Islands / Phi Phi island-hopping tour
landmarks.why: Snorkeling and beach-hopping among Krabi's famous karst islands (Chicken, Tup, Poda, Phi Phi).
landmarks.when: Dry season for calm seas; book early for morning departure
landmarks.how: Longtail or speedboat day tour from Ao Nang pier

landmarks.name: Tiger Cave Temple (Wat Tham Suea)
landmarks.why: 1,260-step climb to a clifftop Buddha with panoramic views over Krabi's jungle and karst landscape.
landmarks.when: Early morning to beat the heat
landmarks.how: Scooter or Grab ~9 km from Krabi Town

landmarks.name: Emerald Pool & Hot Springs (Khlong Thom)
landmarks.why: Spring-fed jade pools and natural warm waterfalls in the Thung Teao forest reserve.
landmarks.how: Scooter or day tour ~1 hr from Ao Nang

weather.hazard: Best months Dec-Mar (dry, calm seas). Southwest monsoon May-Oct brings heavy rain and rough seas; Sep-Oct wettest (~280-300mm/mo) with some island tours cancelled. Hot and humid year-round; no typhoons but monsoon downpours and high humidity.

cities.food: Local Thai dish at a market or roadside spot ~$3 (100 THB); casual sit-down meal for 2 ~$16-22; mid-range dinner for 2 with drinks ~$40-55; big Chang/Leo beer ~$2.50-3.50; coffee at a cafe ~$2.50-3.50. Western/tourist restaurants in Chaweng run notably higher.
cities.transport: No public buses to speak of; scooter rental is the norm at ~$120-170/month (4,000-6,000 THB) plus cheap fuel. Songthaew shared trucks ~$1.50-3 per ride. Grab and Bolt ride-hail work island-wide: short hop ~$3-5, Chaweng to the airport ~$5-8. Taxis are pricey and often metered loosely, so ride-hail apps are better value.
cities.internet: Tourist eSIM (e.g. Airalo/Holafly) ~$4-15 for 5-20GB; local AIS/TrueMove monthly SIM ~$15-25 for big data. 4G/5G is solid around Chaweng, Lamai, Bophut and the ring road; weaker on the mountainous interior and far western beaches. Coworking at Mantra Work Lounge ~$17/day (600 THB), ~$100/week, ~$340/month (12,000 THB); other spaces in Maenam/Fisherman's Village similar.

landmarks.name: Big Buddha (Wat Phra Yai)
landmarks.why: Iconic 12m golden seated Buddha on Koh Faan islet, the island's most recognizable landmark with sea views and a small temple market
landmarks.when: Early morning or late afternoon to avoid midday heat and tour crowds
landmarks.how: Northeast coast near the airport; 15-20 min scooter or Grab from Chaweng

landmarks.name: Ang Thong National Marine Park
landmarks.why: 42-island archipelago of limestone karsts, hidden lagoons and snorkeling; the standout day trip from Samui
landmarks.when: Dry season Jan-Apr for calm seas; boat tours often pause in heavy Nov rains
landmarks.how: Full-day speedboat or big-boat tour from Nathon/Bophut piers
landmarks.cost: Tour ~$45-65/person incl. park fee, lunch, kayak

landmarks.name: Fisherman's Village, Bophut
landmarks.why: Restored old-town lane of wooden Sino-Portuguese shophouses with restaurants, bars and the Friday Walking Street night market
landmarks.when: Friday evening for the walking street; any evening for dining
landmarks.how: North coast; 15 min scooter or Grab from Chaweng
landmarks.cost: Free to wander; street food from ~$2

landmarks.name: Na Muang Waterfalls & viewpoints
landmarks.why: Twin jungle waterfalls with a swimmable lower pool, plus the Lamai coast's Hin Ta & Hin Yai (Grandfather/Grandmother) rocks nearby
landmarks.when: Rainy season (Sep-Dec) for the fullest falls; mornings to beat heat
landmarks.how: Central-south interior; scooter or Grab, ~25-30 min from Chaweng

weather.hazard: Best months mid-Dec to early Mar (driest, clearest); Jan-Apr generally good and hottest in Apr-May. Wettest is the NE-monsoon Oct-Dec, peaking in Nov (~315mm) when seas get rough and marine tours pause. Humidity is high year-round; no typhoon belt but heavy tropical downpours and occasional flooding in Nov.

cities.food: Banh mi from a cart ~$0.80-1.20; bowl of cao lau or pho ~$1.50-2.50; casual local lunch for 2 ~$8-12; mid-range Western/Vietnamese dinner for 2 ~$25-40; local beer (Bia Larue/Saigon) ~$0.80-1.50; cafe coffee (ca phe sua da) ~$1-2, Western espresso ~$2-3.
cities.transport: Town is flat and walkable/cyclable; hotels lend bikes free or rent ~$1-2/day. Motorbike rental ~$5-7/day or ~$50-70/month. Grab/Xanh SM ride-hail widely used: GrabBike short hops ~$0.60-2, GrabCar across town or to Da Nang airport (~30km) ~$12-16. Local taxis metered but Grab usually cheaper.
cities.internet: Local SIM/eSIM (Viettel/Mobifone) unlimited data ~$3-8/month; tourist eSIMs (Airalo/Saily) ~$5-15. Cafe and apartment fiber WiFi is fast and reliable (50-100+ Mbps common). Coworking spaces like Hub Hoi An / Sun Tan run ~$5-8/day or ~$80-120/month; many nomads just work from cafes.

landmarks.name: Hoi An Ancient Town (UNESCO)
landmarks.why: Lantern-lit 15th-19th century trading port; Japanese Covered Bridge, old merchant houses, riverside lanterns at night
landmarks.when: Late afternoon into evening; magical on the monthly full-moon Lantern Festival
landmarks.how: Walk or cycle from anywhere central
landmarks.cost: Town entry ticket ~$5 (120,000 VND) covers 5 heritage sites

landmarks.name: An Bang Beach
landmarks.why: Long sandy beach with relaxed beach bars and seafood shacks, popular with nomads
landmarks.when: Dry season Feb-Aug; mornings to avoid midday heat
landmarks.how: 10-15 min by bike or ~$2-3 GrabBike from Old Town

landmarks.name: My Son Sanctuary
landmarks.why: UNESCO Hindu Cham temple ruins (4th-13th c.) set in a jungle valley
landmarks.when: Early morning to beat heat and crowds
landmarks.how: ~40km west; tour ~$15-20 or private car ~$30-40 round trip

landmarks.name: Tra Que Vegetable Village & rice paddies
landmarks.why: Cycle through organic herb gardens and emerald rice fields; cooking classes and farm visits

weather.hazard: Best months Feb-May (warm, dry, low rain). Oct-Nov is the wettest with peak monsoon and frequent flooding of the Old Town; Sep-Dec brings typhoon/storm risk off the East Sea. Jun-Aug is hot and humid but mostly dry.

cities.food: Bun cha or banh mi street meal $1.50-3; casual local dinner for 2 ~$10-14; mid-range 3-course dinner for 2 ~$21; fresh-seafood splurge for 2 $30-45. Draught beer $0.60-1, bottled craft $2; cappuccino $1.75, Vietnamese iced coffee (ca phe sua da) $0.80-1.20.
cities.transport: Walkable beachfront core. Grab bike $1-2 short hop, Grab car $2-4 across town; rented scooter $5-7/day or ~$50/month plus cheap petrol. Airport (CXR, ~35km south) by Grab car ~$15-18 or shared shuttle bus ~$2-3. No metro; taxis (Mai Linh/Vinasun) metered.
cities.internet: Home fiber 60+ Mbps ~$9/month; mobile data is fast and cheap (Viettel/Mobifone tourist eSIM ~$5-8 for 30 days with generous data). Coworking is limited but present (e.g. iGo, Maac, cafe coworking) ~$70-100/month for a hot desk; many cafes have reliable free wifi.

landmarks.name: Po Nagar Cham Towers
landmarks.why: 8th-13th century Cham Hindu temple complex on a hill over the Cai River, Nha Trang's top cultural site
landmarks.when: Early morning 8-9am to beat heat and crowds
landmarks.how: Grab bike/car ~$2 from center (3km north), or scooter

landmarks.name: VinWonders & Vinpearl Cable Car (Hon Tre Island)
landmarks.why: Large theme/water park reached by a scenic over-sea cable car; full day of rides and beaches
landmarks.when: Weekday to avoid crowds; arrive at opening
landmarks.how: Cable car or speedboat from the dedicated terminal south of town

landmarks.name: Island-hopping & scuba/snorkel trip
landmarks.why: Nha Trang is Vietnam's diving capital; clear bay waters, coral, and beach islands (Hon Mun marine park)
landmarks.when: Feb-Aug for calm seas and best visibility
landmarks.how: Boat tours depart Cau Da pier; book via dive shops or hotel

landmarks.name: Long Son Pagoda & White Buddha
landmarks.why: Hilltop temple crowned by a giant seated white Buddha with panoramic city views
landmarks.when: Late afternoon for cooler light and views
landmarks.how: Grab/scooter ~$1-2 from center, then short stair climb

weather.hazard: Best months Feb-Aug (dry, sunny, calm sea). Oct-Nov is peak rainy season with the wettest months (300-330mm) and flood/storm risk; Sep-Dec is the NE monsoon with occasional typhoons/tropical depressions hitting the central coast. Hot and muggy May-Aug; Jan-Feb is the coolest and driest.

cities.food: Street nasi goreng or gudeg 15,000-25,000 IDR ($1-1.60); casual sit-down meal for 2 ~$8-12; mid-range dinner for 2 at a nicer warung/restaurant ~$20-30; large Bintang beer ~$2.50-3.50; local coffee/kopi ~$1, cafe latte ~$2-2.50.
cities.transport: Gojek/Grab dominate: motorbike taxi ~$0.70-1.50 across town, car ~$2-4. Rent a scooter ~$4-5/day or ~$60/month. TransJogja bus ~$0.25/ride. Becak (pedicab) short hops $1-2 after haggling. Most rides in the compact center are very cheap.
cities.internet: Tourist eSIM (Telkomsel/Holafly type) ~$7 for 5GB/week, ~$15 for 20GB; local Telkomsel SIM ~$5-10/month. Fiber in apartments is common and fast. Coworking widely available: day pass ~$3-6, monthly hot desk ~$60-100; cafes with free wifi everywhere.

landmarks.name: Borobudur Temple
landmarks.why: World's largest Buddhist temple, a 9th-century UNESCO masterpiece of stone reliefs and stupas; iconic sunrise views over volcanoes and jungle.
landmarks.when: Early morning (sunrise) to beat heat and crowds; dry season May-Sep best.
landmarks.how: About 1 hr drive NW; Grab/car ~$20-30 round trip, or organized tour ~$25-40 pp.
landmarks.cost: Foreigner entry ~$25 (temple grounds); climbing the temple structure timed-slot ~$30 extra.

landmarks.name: Prambanan Temple
landmarks.why: Towering 9th-century Hindu temple complex (UNESCO) with dramatic spires dedicated to Shiva; stunning at sunset and for the Ramayana ballet.
landmarks.when: Late afternoon for sunset light; combine with evening Ramayana dance show.
landmarks.how: ~17 km east; Grab/car ~$5-8 each way or TransJogja bus.

landmarks.name: Kraton (Sultan's Palace) & Taman Sari
landmarks.why: Living royal palace of the Yogyakarta Sultanate plus the atmospheric Water Castle ruins; the cultural heart of the city.
landmarks.when: Morning, when gamelan/dance performances are often held and before midday heat.
landmarks.how: Central, walkable or short Grab ride from most accommodation.
landmarks.cost: Each site ~$1-3 entry, plus small camera fee.

landmarks.name: Malioboro Street & Beringharjo Market
landmarks.why: The bustling main shopping and street-food artery; batik, souvenirs, and Jogja's famous nightlife of street musicians and lesehan dining.
landmarks.when: Late afternoon into evening when it comes alive and cools down.
landmarks.cost: Free to stroll; budget for street food and shopping.

weather.hazard: Best months Jun-Sep (dry season, low rain, comfortable). Wet/monsoon season Nov-Apr peaks Jan-Mar with heavy afternoon downpours and high humidity. No typhoons (near equator). Year-round heat 30-32C; volcanic ash possible during Mt Merapi activity.

cities.food: Local carinderia meal ~$2.50-4; casual sit-down dinner for two ~$15-22; mid-range restaurant dinner for two ~$30-45; local beer (San Miguel) ~$1.30-2; coffee at a cafe ~$2.50-3.50. Lechon (roast pork) is the Cebu specialty.
cities.transport: Jeepneys ~$0.25 min fare (PHP 14), modern jeepneys ~$0.30. Grab is the go-to ride-hail: short city hop ~$2.50-4, cross-town ~$5-8, airport (Mactan) to city ~$6-11. MyBus airport shuttle to SM ~$1-2. Traffic is heavy at peak hours; motorbike-style habal-habal exists but Grab is safer for nomads.
cities.internet: Home fiber ~$25-40/mo (PLDT/Converge/Globe). Tourist eSIM (Airalo/local Globe-Smart) ~$5-15 for 5-20GB. Coworking is solid in IT Park and Cebu Business Park: day pass ~$8-15, monthly hot desk ~$100-150 (The Tide, Ete Co., Company spaces). Mall and cafe wifi widely available.

landmarks.name: Magellan's Cross & Basilica del Santo Niño
landmarks.why: Cebu's historic-religious heart: the 1521 cross marking the arrival of Christianity in the Philippines, beside the country's oldest church and the revered Santo Niño icon.
landmarks.when: Year-round; mornings to avoid heat and crowds. January for the Sinulog festival.
landmarks.how: Central downtown Cebu; short Grab ride (~$2-3) or jeepney from most areas.

landmarks.name: Kawasan Falls (Badian)
landmarks.why: Iconic turquoise multi-tier waterfalls and the launch point for the famous canyoneering adventure through Matutinao River.
landmarks.when: Dry season Jan-May for best water clarity; go early to beat day-trip crowds.
landmarks.how: ~3 hours south by car/bus; most do an organized canyoneering day tour from Cebu City.
landmarks.cost: Entrance ~$1.50; canyoneering tour ~$30-55 pp incl. gear/guide

landmarks.name: Oslob whale shark watching & Moalboal
landmarks.why: Swim alongside whale sharks at Oslob and snorkel the famous sardine run and turtles at Moalboal's Panagsama reef.
landmarks.when: Whale sharks year-round (early morning best); sardine run reliable most of the year.
landmarks.how: South coast, ~3 hours by bus/van; combine as a 1-2 day trip from the city.
landmarks.cost: Whale shark watch+snorkel ~$18-30; sardine run snorkel ~$5-10

landmarks.name: Temple of Leah & Sirao Garden (Busay hills)
landmarks.why: A grand Roman-style hilltop temple with city views, paired with the colorful 'Little Amsterdam' celosia flower garden in the cool uplands.
landmarks.when: Late afternoon for cooler air and sunset over Cebu City.
landmarks.how: Busay/Transcentral Highway above the city; ~30-45 min Grab (~$8-12 round trip with wait).

weather.hazard: Best months: Feb-May (driest, sunny). Wettest June-November with peak rain around July-October; typhoon risk runs roughly June-December though Cebu is somewhat sheltered versus Luzon. Hot and humid year-round (highs 30-33C); March-May is the hottest stretch.

cities.food: Local silog/carinderia meal ~$3-5; casual dinner for 2 (grilled fish, rice, veg) ~$14-20; mid beachfront/Western dinner for 2 ~$35-50; San Miguel beer ~$2; coffee ~$2.50-3.50. Tourist-strip prices run notably higher than mainland Philippines.
cities.transport: Tricycles dominate: town to Corong-Corong ~PHP 50-100 ($1-2), short hops ~PHP 15-20pp shared. No Grab/ride-hail in El Nido; negotiate or use hotel transfers. Shared van Puerto Princesa-El Nido (5-6h) ~PHP 700 ($12)pp. Motorbike rental ~PHP 500-700/day ($9-12).
cities.internet: Globe is the usable network (~10-20 Mbps LTE); Smart is weak. Tourist eSIMs (Airalo/Holafly) ~$5-10 for a few GB. WiFi is patchy and slow town-wide; Lio Beach and Corong-Corong have better fiber. Coworking: Lexias El Nido and Neighbors & Nomads offer reliable work-grade WiFi (~PHP 300-500/day).

landmarks.name: Big Lagoon & Small Lagoon (Bacuit Bay)
landmarks.why: El Nido's signature karst-cliff lagoons with turquoise water, kayaking, and snorkeling - the reason most people come.
landmarks.when: Dec-May dry season, early morning to beat crowds and afternoon wind.
landmarks.how: Tour A island-hopping boat from El Nido town beach; kayak rental extra inside Big Lagoon.

landmarks.name: Nacpan Beach
landmarks.why: Long, uncrowded twin golden-sand beach with a viewpoint - the best mainland beach near town.
landmarks.when: Year-round, best on dry-season afternoons; great for sunset.
landmarks.how: Tricycle or van from town (~45 min), or rent a motorbike.
landmarks.cost: Tricycle round trip ~PHP 800-1,200 ($14-21) for 2; small entrance/parking fee

landmarks.name: Las Cabanas Beach & zipline
landmarks.why: Closest swimmable sunset beach to town with beach bars and an island-to-beach zipline.
landmarks.when: Late afternoon for sunset, dry season.

landmarks.name: Taraw Cliff Canopy Walk / viewpoint
landmarks.why: Via ferrata climb up the limestone cliff over El Nido town for panoramic Bacuit Bay views.
landmarks.how: Guided via ferrata booked in town; harness and guide provided.

weather.hazard: Best months Dec-Apr (dry, calm seas, reliable island-hopping). May is hot and transitional. Jun-Oct is the SW monsoon (habagat) with heavy rain, peaking Jul-Sep; tours and boats are frequently cancelled. Typhoons can affect the region mainly Aug-Nov, though Palawan is hit less often than the rest of the Philippines.
